overview.summary: The FLORAHOX group executed a simple attack chain targeting transportation systems by first compromising employee credentials through phishing, then exploiting outdated IoT devices to gain control of critical infrastructure, ultimately causing disruptions to traffic control systems.

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.kev: CVE-2022-42827 [CWE-XXX]: Suggested vulnerability for phishing attacks
stage.description: Attackers sent spear-phished emails containing malicious links to transportation system employees, exploiting CVE-2022-42827 to trick victims into entering credentials for admin portals, enabling initial access to internal networks.

stage.name: Execution
stage.kev: CVE-2020-9907 [CWE-XXX]: Exploitation of unpatchable firmware vulnerabilities
stage.capec: CAPEC ID: 682: Exploitation of Firmware or ROM Code with Unpatchable Vulnerabilities
stage.description: Using credentials obtained from phishing, attackers exploited CVE-2020-9907 in end-of-life IoT devices (e.g., routers) with default credentials to gain remote control over traffic signal systems.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: Attackers overwhelmed traffic control systems with illegitimate traffic, reconfiguring traffic lights and train signals to cause gridlock and potential derailments, leveraging compromised network nodes for persistent disruption.

Scenario.summary: The FLORAHOX campaign targeted transportation systems through a straightforward attack chain: phishing for credentials, exploiting unpatched IoT devices, and finally disrupting critical infrastructure through denial-of-service techniques.
